zotero.abstractNote: Automated inspection tasks require a sequence of sensor poses to cover the surface of an object completely. This problem is known as the Next-Best-View (NBV) planning problem. While classical approaches rely on geometric heuristics, we propose a reinforcement learning (RL) approach to learn a policy for view planning. We formulate the NBV problem as a Markov decision process and train a deep Q-network to select the next sensor pose. To handle the continuous state and action spaces, we use a discretized representation of the object surface and the sensor poses. We evaluate our approach in simulation on a set of CAD models and compare it to a state-of-the-art heuristic approach. Our results show that the RL agent learns to plan efficient inspection sequences and outperforms the heuristic approach in terms of coverage and path length.
